data.title: Tracking Philippe Laffont's $40B Coatue Management Portfolio - Q4 2025 Update
data.description:
This article is part of a series that provides an ongoing analysis of the changes made to Coatue Management's 13F stock portfolio on a quarterly basis. It is based on Coatue's regulatory 13F form filed on 02/17/2026. The 13F portfolio value decreased from $40.79B to $39.96B this quarter. Recent 13F reports have shown around 75 individual stock positions in the portfolio. The largest five stakes are Alphabet, Taiwan Semi, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Amazon. They add up to ~32% of the entire portfolio. Please visit our Tracking Philippe Laffont's Coatue Portfolio series to get an idea of their investment philosophy and our previous update for the fund's moves during Q3 2025.

Philippe Laffont was a telecom analyst at Tiger Management from 1996. He founded Coatue Management as a tech-focused long-short hedge fund in 1999 and is one of the most successful among the "tiger cubs". To know more about Julian Robertson and his legendary Tiger Management, check out Julian Robertson: A Tiger in the Land of Bulls and Bears.

Note: In May, Coatue Management launched Coatue Innovative Strategies Fund (CTEK), a closed-end fund with a $50K minimum and access only to accredited investors.

Stake Disposals

Alibaba Group Holding (BABA): The BABA stake was built during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$80 and ~$148. The next quarter saw the position sold down by ~80% at prices between ~$96 and ~$135. There was a ~130% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$104 and ~$182. The disposal this quarter was at prices between ~$146 and ~$193. The stock currently trades at ~$125.

CoreWeave (CRWV): CRWV had an IPO in March. Shares started trading at ~$40 and currently go for $80.45. Coatue's position goes back to a Series C funding round in May 2024. There was a ~25% stake increase during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$33.50 and ~$187. The last quarter saw ~63% selling at prices between ~$84 and ~$166. The elimination this quarter was at prices between ~$64 and ~$153.

KKR & Co. (KKR): The 0.71% KKR stake was established during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$104 and ~$133. There was a ~20% stake increase during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$105 and ~$170. The last quarter saw a ~40% reduction in prices between ~$127 and ~$154. The position was sold this quarter at prices between ~$112 and ~$145. The stock is now at ~$90.

Atlassian Corp. (TEAM), Hinge Health (HNGE), and Intuit Inc. (INTU): These stakes were dropped during the quarter.

Stake Increases

Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM): The 6.56% TSM position was built during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$99 and ~$149. The next quarter saw a ~10% stake increase at prices between ~$127 and ~$180. There was ~18% selling during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$147 and ~$190. Q1 2025 saw a ~20% reduction at prices between ~$160 and ~$226. The stock is now at ~$326. There was a minor ~5% stake increase this quarter.

Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): MSFT is 6.26% of the portfolio stake purchased in Q3 2021 at prices between ~$277 and ~$305. Recent activity follows. The two quarters through Q1 2025 saw a ~17% trimming, while the next quarter saw a ~20% stake increase at prices between ~$345 and ~$501. That was followed by a similar increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$489 and ~$556. This quarter also saw a ~11% stake increase at prices between ~$465 and ~$554. The stock currently trades at ~$366.

Amazon (AMZN): A large stake in AMZN was built in the 2009-2011 timeframe but was sold down the next year. A similar stake build-up happened in H2 2013 at prices between ~$13 and ~$14. The next quarter saw an ~80% reduction in prices between ~$14 and ~$16. The stake has wavered. Recent activity follows. The three quarters through Q2 2023 saw a huge stake build-up at prices between ~$82 and ~$130. The next quarter saw ~15% selling at prices between ~$126 and ~$145. Q4 2023 also saw a ~7% trimming, while Q2 2024 saw a similar increase. That was followed by a ~14% selling during the last quarter at prices between ~$211 and ~$239. This quarter saw a ~14% stake increase at prices between ~$211 and ~$259. The stock is now at ~$208, and it is now at 5.74% of the portfolio.

Constellation Energy (CEG): CEG is a 5.22% stake established during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$111 and ~$187. The next quarter saw a ~140% stake increase at prices between ~$180 and ~$231. Q3 2024 saw a ~60% stake increase at prices between ~$167 and ~$263. There was a ~14% selling during the next quarter at prices between ~$224 and ~$285. The stock currently trades at ~$295. There was a ~15% trimming over the last three quarters, while in this quarter there was a ~3% stake increase.

Applied Materials (AMAT): The 3.85% AMAT stake was established during the last quarter at prices between ~$155 and ~$211. This quarter saw an ~80% stake increase at prices between ~$203 and ~$276. The stock is now well above those ranges at ~$339.

Spotify Technology (SPOT): SPOT is 3.56% of the portfolio position purchased during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$290 and ~$384. The next quarter saw a two-thirds increase at prices between ~$363 and ~$502. There was a ~22% stake increase during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$443 and ~$653. The last quarter saw a ~13% further increase at prices between ~$616 and ~$770. This quarter saw another ~35% stake increase at prices between ~$549 and ~$722. The stock is now at ~$478.

Netflix, Inc. (NFLX): The 2.55% NFLX position is a very long-term stake. It was established in 2012 at very low prices. The stake has wavered. Recent activity follows. Q2 2024 saw ~42% selling at prices between ~$55 and ~$69. There was a ~14% stake increase during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$69 and ~$94. The next quarter saw an ~11% trimming at prices between ~$82 and ~$107. This quarter saw a ~60% stake increase at prices between ~$91 and ~$125. The stock is now at $93.32.

Note: The prices quoted above are adjusted for the 10-for-1 stock split in November 2025.

DoorDash (DASH): The 2.47% DASH stake was established during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$100 and ~$144. The next quarter saw a one-third stake increase at prices between ~$142 and ~$181. Q1 2025 saw the position sold down by ~37% at prices between ~$166 and ~$215, while during the next quarter there was a one-third increase at prices between ~$155 and ~$247. That was followed by a ~12% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$232 and ~$278. This quarter saw another ~80% stake increase at prices between ~$182 and ~$286. The stock currently trades at ~$152.

Natera, Inc. (NTRA): NTRA is 2.55% of the portfolio position primarily built this quarter at prices between ~$157 and ~$247. The stock currently trades at ~$196.

Snowflake Inc. (SNOW): The 1.36% SNOW position was purchased during the last quarter at prices between ~$188 and ~$250. There was a ~30% stake increase this quarter at prices between ~$212 and ~$281. It now goes for ~$162.

Arm Holdings (ARM): The 0.63% ARM stake was purchased during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$80 and ~$168. The last quarter saw a ~50% reduction in prices between ~$127 and ~$167. The stock is now at ~$155. There was a marginal increase this quarter.

Adobe (ADBE) and Intuitive Surgical (ISRG): These two positions were increased this quarter.

Stake Decreases

Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL): The large (top three) ~7% GOOGL stake was primarily built during the last quarter at prices between ~$173 and ~$256. The stock currently trades above that range at ~$281. There was a ~5% trimming this quarter.

Meta Platforms (META): META is currently at 6.25% of the portfolio. It was first purchased during Q3 2013 at prices between ~$24 and ~$51. The stake has wavered. Recent activity follows. The two quarters through Q1 2023 saw a huge ~480% stake build-up at prices between ~$89 and ~$212. There was a ~30% selling during Q2 2023 at prices between ~$208 and ~$289. The stake was decreased by 23% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$344 and ~$512. There was a ~10% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$691 and ~$796. The stock currently trades at ~$548. This quarter saw a ~6% trimming.

GE Vernova (GEV): GEV was spun off from GE in April 2024. Shares started trading at ~$137 and currently go for ~$873. The 5.51% of the portfolio stake was established during Q2 2024 as it traded between ~$123 and ~$182. The next quarter saw an ~18% stake increase at prices between ~$160 and ~$256. The last five quarters saw minor trimming.

Broadcom Inc. (AVGO): AVGO is 4.77% of the portfolio stake established during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$105 and ~$140. The next quarter saw a one-third reduction in prices between ~$120 and ~$182. Q3 2024 saw the stake increased by ~50% at prices between ~$136 and ~$178. There was an ~18% selling during the next quarter at prices between ~$159 and ~$249. Q2 2025 saw a ~60% stake increase at prices between ~$138 and ~$278. The stock is now at ~$309. The last two quarters saw only minor adjustments.

Nvidia Corp. (NVDA): NVDA is now at 4.30% of the portfolio. Q4 2021 had seen a ~200% stake increase at prices between ~$20 and ~$33.50. Recent activity follows. The stake was decreased by 68% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$48 and ~$95. Q3 2024 saw another ~26% selling at prices between ~$99 and ~$135. That was followed by a ~15% reduction during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$104 and ~$153. The next quarter saw a ~35% stake increase at prices between ~$87 and ~$159. There was a ~14% reduction during the last quarter at prices between ~$152 and ~$187. The stock is now at ~$171. There was a minor ~7% trimming this quarter.

Lam Research (LRCX): LRCX is 4.20% of the portfolio position established during Q3 2022 at prices between ~$37 and ~$53. Recent activity follows. The stake was decreased by 35% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$73 and ~$99. Q1 2025 saw the position doubled at prices between ~$70 and ~$92. There was a ~28% stake increase during the next quarter at prices between ~$56 and ~$98. The stock currently trades at ~$212. The last two quarters saw minor trimming.

Note: The prices quoted for LRCX are adjusted for the 10-for-1 stock split during October 2024.

Eaton Corp. (ETN): ETN is 3.76% of the portfolio position purchased during Q2 2023 at prices between ~$156 and ~$201. The position was increased by 113% in the next quarter at prices between ~$196 and ~$237. The stock is now at ~$357. The last few quarters saw only minor adjustments.

Reddit, Inc. (RDDT): RDDT is 3.19% of the portfolio position purchased during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$66 and ~$178. There was a ~27% reduction during the next quarter at prices between ~$98 and ~$230, while during Q2 2025 there was a ~30% stake increase at prices between ~$80 and ~$155. The stock currently trades at ~$127. The last two quarters saw minor trimming.

Oracle Corp. (ORCL): ORCL is 2.17% of the portfolio position established during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$119 and ~$228. There was a ~25% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$216 and ~$346. The stock currently trades at ~$143. This quarter saw a ~7% trimming.

AppLovin Corp. (APP), Carvana (CVNA), Caris Life Sciences (CAI), Chime Financial (CHYM), First Citizens BancShares (FCNCA), Nu Holdings (NU), PayPal Holdings (PYPL), Synopsys, Inc. (SNPS), and Tesla, Inc. (TSLA): These stakes were reduced during the quarter.

Kept Steady

Advanced Micro Devices (AMD): The small 0.66% stake in AMD was kept steady this quarter.

data.title: Lithium Junior Miners News For The Month Of March 2026
data.description:
Welcome to the March 2026 edition of the 'junior' lithium miner news. We have categorized those lithium miners that are not in commercial production as the juniors.

Note: Investors are reminded that many of the lithium juniors will most likely not be needed until the late 2020's (& 2030's) to supply the potentially booming electric vehicle [EV] and battery energy stationary storage [BESS] markets. This means investing in these companies requires a higher risk tolerance and a longer time frame.

The past month China lithium carbonate spot prices and spodumene prices were generally flat.

Lithium price news

Metal.com reported a Australian spodumene concentrate 6% Li2O CIF China spot price of USD 2,080, as of March 24, 2026, roughly flat the past month.

The China lithium carbonate spot price was flat last month, now at CNY 152,500.

China lithium carbonate spot price 5 year chart - CNY 152,500 (~USD 22,096) (source)

Katusa Research is forecasting a small lithium deficit to begin in 2026 (as of January 2026) (source)

RMIS forecasts lithium deficits to start after 2028, may grow very large without major new investments (as of May 2025) (source)

GGII, a Shenzhen-based consultancy, forecasts a balanced lithium market this year, a 10,000t LCE deficit in 2026, and a 60,000t deficit in 2027 (as of mid 2025) (source)

Lithium market news

For a summary of the latest lithium market news and the 'major' lithium company's news, investors can read: "Lithium Miners News For The Month Of March 2026" article. Highlights include:

  • Lithium stocks rise as Zimbabwe bans exports of lithium concentrates.
  • New Chinese breakthrough in lithium battery technology enables 700 Wh/kg energy density, using fluorinated hydrocarbon solvents.
  • UK enters commercial lithium production with geothermal plant launch in Cornwall, initial production is set at 100 tons per year.
  • Bloomberg: Lithium recovery has legs as demand outlook improves, SQM says.
  • Fastmarkets: Tightening supply expectations combined with macroeconomic uncertainty causes volatility. A tighter lithium market in early 2026, with demand outpacing supply.
  • CATL's 2025 net income climbed 42% YoT, to 72.2 billion yuan ($10.4B).
  • LG says it will supply Tesla with $4.3B of LFP batteries for BESS.
  • Albemarle forecasts global energy storage system production to grow 25 to 60% year over year in 2026.

Junior lithium miners company news

Galan Lithium [ASX:GLN] (GLNLF)

Galan is developing their flagship Hombre Muerto West ("HMW") Lithium Project located on the west side edge of the high grade, low impurity Hombre Muerto Salar in Argentina.

On March 2 Galan Lithium announced:

HMW Phase 1 construction update - 92% complete...

  • Nanofiltration plant assembly progressing to plan.
  • Construction expected to complete in the short term, transitioning to testing and commissioning.
  • Nanofiltration plant commissioning with raw brine expected to follow successful testing.
  • Significant brine inventory ready for processing.
  • First lithium chloride concentrate in H1 2026, first shipment H2 2026.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • H1, 2026 - Commence production and then ramp to 5.2ktpa LCE of lithium chloride production. Phase 2 to follow and ramp to 20Ktpa LCE.

Galan Lithium HMW site showing the nanofiltration plant (source)

LibertyStream Infrastructure Partners Inc. [TSXV:LIB]

On March 12 LibertyStream announced:

LibertyStream begins installation and integration of DLE extraction and refining systems at Select Water Solutions’ site; Initial production expected early Q2...this initial deployment is intended to support continued bulk sample production and customer qualification while establishing the systems-integration template for planned scaling at the site and future deployments across major U.S. basins, including the Permian and Bakken.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • 2026 - Start of small scale commercial production from Permian Basin oilfield brine.
  • 2027 - Targeting commercial volumes beginning in 2027.

Atlas Lithium Corp. (ATLX)

No news for the month.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • 2026 - Production targeted to begin at the Neves Lithium Project (a part of the Minas Gerais Lithium Project) in Brazil.

Standard Lithium [TSXV:SLI] (SLI)

On March 9 Standard Lithium announced:

Smackover Lithium signs first binding customer offtake agreement for the South West Arkansas Project...with Trafigura Trading LLC...Under the terms of this binding take-or-pay offtake agreement (the “Agreement”), the JV will supply Trafigura with 8,000 metric tonnes per year of battery-quality lithium carbonate over a 10-year period, beginning at the start of commercial production.

Lithium Ionic Corp. [TSXV:LTH] (OTCQB:LTHCF)

On March 25 Lithium Ionic announced:

Lithium Ionic secures offtake agreements with leading integrated lithium producers, including one of the world’s largest lithium hydroxide producers, for Bandeira Project production.

  • Five-year agreements with US$1,000t floor price, no discount to spot pricing and full upside exposure to support Project financing.
  • Lithium Ionic will supply a combined 170,000 tonnes per annum of spodumene concentrate under five-year binding take-or-pay offtake agreements to Yahua Group and Grand Chen, two integrated counterparties serving tier-one battery and EV manufacturers, including BYD and other leading OEMs..
  • US$20 million combined pre-payment facility associated with the offtake agreements, aligned with the Project’s advancement toward construction.

Lithium Americas [TSX:LAC] (LAC)

Lithium Americas has the Thacker Pass JV Project (LAC 62%, GM 38%, the U.S. DoE also holds a 5% Project stake via warrants) and ~5.2% equity in GT1.

On March 19 Lithium Americas announced:

Full Year 2025 results... 2025 marked a transformative year for Thacker Pass. Construction is advancing at full pace, and we are carrying that strong momentum into 2026... “Construction at Thacker Pass is progressing rapidly"...We remain on track for mechanical completion of Phase 1 in late 2027.

Upcoming catalysts:

  • 2026 - Thacker Pass Phase 1 construction to progress.
  • Late 2027 - Phase 1 (40,000tpa LCE) production from Thacker Pass Nevada (plan to eventually ramp to 160,000tpa in four phases of 40,000 tpa each at 4 year intervals starting from late 2027).

Vulcan Energy Resources [ASX: VUL] (OTCPK:VULNF)

Vulcan Energy Resources state that they have "the largest lithium resource in Europe" with a total of 15.85mt LCE, at an average lithium grade of 181 mg/L. The Company is in the development stage developing a geothermal lithium brine operation (geothermal energy plus lithium extraction plants) in the Upper Rhine Valley of Germany.

On March 17 Vulcan Energy Resources announced:

Vulcan secures lithium production licence for Lionheart...which is now funded and in construction. It is the first such licence to be granted in the Upper Rhine Valley Brine Field (URVBF), and in the state of Rhineland-Palatinate. Lionheart involves the construction of an integrated lithium and renewable energy project targeting production capacity of 24,000 tonnes of lithium hydroxide monohydrate (LHM), enough for ca. 500,000 electric vehicle batteries per annum, with a co-product of 275 GWh of renewable power and 560 GWh of renewable heat per annum for local consumers, over an estimated 30-year project life.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • 2028 - Target to commence commercial production of 24,000tpa LiOH monohydrate in Phase One of the Lionheart Project in Germany, then ramp to 40,000tpa.

Atlantic Lithium Limited [LSE:ALL] [ASX:A11] (OTCQX:ALLIF)

Atlantic Lithium is working to progress its Ewoyaa JV Project in Ghana towards production.

On March 17 Atlantic Lithium announced:

Atlantic Lithium to raise up to c. US$16.4m through a Strategic Investment from Ghanaian investors and a third placement with Long State Investments Ltd.

On March 20 Atlantic Lithium announced:

Parliamentary Ratification of Ewoyaa Mining Lease. Parliament of Ghana issues official approval of the Mining Lease in respect of the Ewoyaa Lithium Project, representing a significant de-risking milestone...Under the terms of the Mining Lease, which was initially granted by Ghana's Ministry of Lands and Natural Resources in October 2023, Atlantic Lithium has the exclusive rights to carry out mining and commercial production activities over the Mining Lease area for an initial 15-year period, renewable in accordance with Ghanaian legislation...The revised terms comprise the alignment of the Project's royalty rate and Growth and Sustainability Levy to current legislated rates in Ghana.

Ghana royalties scale (source)

Frontier Lithium [TSXV:FL] (OTCQX:LITOF)

Frontier Lithium own the PAK Lithium (Spodumene) Project comprising 26,774 hectares and located 175 kilometers north of Red Lake in northwestern Ontario. The PAK deposit is a lithium-cesium-tantalum [LCT] type pegmatite containing high-purity, technical-grade spodumene (below 0.1% iron oxide). JV with Mitsubishi Corporation to advance the Project signed in 2024.

On March 2 Frontier Lithium announced:

Frontier Lithium signs memorandum of understanding with Panasonic Energy to advance north American battery supply chain. Under the MoU, Panasonic Energy has expressed interest in procuring lithium hydroxide from the PAK Lithium Project. The parties also intend to explore broader collaboration opportunities to advance a resilient and sustainable battery supply chain in North America, including potential technical cooperation. The parties to the MoU have confirmed their willingness to negotiate a definitive offtake agreement at an appropriate stage of the Project.

On March 9 Frontier Lithium announced:

Frontier Lithium recognized under G7 critical minerals production alliance and federal permitting coordination and grants stock options.

Savannah Resources [LSE:SAV] [GR:SAV] (OTCPK:SAVNF)

On March 23 Savannah Resources announced:

Project Timetable Update. DFS and RECAPE on track for summer 2026 completion...reiterates the goal of first production in 2028.

ioneer Ltd [ASX:INR] [GR:4G1] (IONR)

ioneer Ltd. 100% owns its flagship Rhyolite Ridge Lithium-Boron Project in Nevada, USA.

On March 18 ioneer announced:

Annual Report to shareholders for the six months ended 31 December 2025...Our achievements during this reporting period demonstrate Ioneer’s continued focus on advancing the strategic partnering process and progressing Rhyolite Ridge towards making a Final Investment Decision [FID]...We continue to advance project-related work following the closing of a $996 million loan from the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Energy Dominance Financing in January 2025.

Critical Elements Lithium Corp. [TSXV:CRE] [GR:F12] (OTCQX:CRECF)

No news for the month.

Critical Metals Corp (CRML) ("CRML") - Majority shareholder is European Lithium Ltd [ASX:EUR] (OTCQB:EULIF)

CRML owns the "fully licensed" Wolfsberg Lithium Project in Austria. They also own the Tanbreez Rare Earth Project in Greenland.

On March 16 European Lithium announced: "31 December 2025 Half Year Report."

Winsome Resources Limited [ASX:WR1] [FSE:4XJ] (OTCQB:WRSLF) - Takeover planned by Li-FT Power - likely to finalize in May 2026

On March 26 Winsome Resources announced:

Court approves convening of Scheme Meetings and distribution of Scheme Booklet...The Scheme Meetings will be held in person on 5 May 2026.

PMET Resources Inc. [TSX:PMET][ASX:PMT] (OTCQX:PMETF) - formerly Patriot Battery Metals

PMET Resources own the Shaakichiuwaanaan Project (formerly named the Corvette Lithium Project) in James Bay, Quebec.

On March 18 PMET Resources announced:

Widest Caesium drill intercept to date at the Helios Caesium Zone in final batch of 2025 drill results from Shaakichiuwaanaan...4.9 m at 3.65% Cs2O.

Global Lithium Resources [ASX:GL1] (OTCPK:GBLRF)

On March 3 Global Lithium Resources announced:

Manna Lithium Project continues to advance with submission of MDCP and key project appointments...the Mining Development and Closure Proposal (MDCP) for GL1’s Manna Lithium Project has been lodged with the Department of Mines, Petroleum and Exploration (DMPE) in Western Australia.

Delta Lithium [ASX:DLI] (formerly Red Dirt Metals)

On March 10 Delta Lithium announced:

Half Yearly Report and Accounts...During the half year ended 31 December 2025, Delta focussed on its two key projects in Western Australia. At Mt Ida, Delta successfully completed the demerger of its Mt Ida Gold Project into a new, standalone entity, named Ballard Mining Limited (Ballard).

European Metals Holdings [ASX:EMH] [AIM:EMH] [GR:E861] (OTCQX:EMHLF)(OTCQX:EMHXY)

No significant news for the month.

Lithium South Development Corp. [TSXV:LIS] (OTCQB:LISMF) - Share Purchase Agreement with POSCO Argentina (expected to finalize in March 2026)

No news for the month.

Q2 Metals Corp. [TSXV:QTWO] (QUEXF) (QTWO)

On March 23 Q2 Metals announced:

Multiple 200+ metre intervals of continuous spodumene pegmatite and provides an update on the inaugural Mineral Resource estimate at the Cisco Lithium Project, Quebec, Canada...Drill hole CS25-071 encountered eight (8) spodumene pegmatite intervals, with the widest continuous interval of 264.6 m, followed by an additional 152.9 m of continuous spodumene pegmatite...The Company advises that completion of the Mineral Resource Estimate (“MRE”) has been extended to allow for additional geological modelling work...The Exploration Target estimated a range of potential lithium mineralization of 215 to 329 million tonnes at a grade ranging from 1.0 to 1.38% Li2O, based only on the first 40 holes drilled.

Century Lithium Corp. (TSXV:LCE) (OTCQX:CYDVF)(Formerly Cypress Development Corp.)

Century Lithium Corp. is focused on developing its Angel Island Project (formerly called Clayton Valley Lithium Project) in west-central Nevada.

On March 16 Century Lithium announced: "Century Lithium closes C$7,000,000 Brokered Life Financing."

Lake Resources NL [ASX:LKE] [GR:LK1] (OTCQB:LLKKF)

Lake Resources own the Kachi Lithium Brine Project in Argentina. Lake has been working with Lilac Solutions Technology (private, and backed by Bill Gates) for direct lithium extraction and rapid lithium processing.

No news for the month; however there was a CEO interview video.

AVZ Minerals

AVZ Minerals owns 75% of its Manono Lithium & Tin Project in the DRC. The Project ownership is currently in dispute. No longer ASX listed.

No news for the month.

E3 Lithium Ltd. [TSXV:ETL] [FSE:OW3] (OTCQX:EEMMF)

E3 Lithium Ltd. is a lithium development company focused on commercializing its extraction technology and advancing the world's 7th largest lithium resource with operations in Alberta. E3 has a M&I Resource of 16.0Mt.

On March 2 E3 Lithium announced:

E3 Lithium receives conditional approval for up to C$36.5 million in Federal Government funding to accelerate its Clearwater Project.

On March 9 E3 Lithium announced:

E3 Lithium provides activity update on Phase Two of its Demonstration Facility and details employee incentives for 2026.

American Lithium Corp. [TSXV:LI] [FSE:5LA1] (AMLIF)

No news for the month.

Wealth Minerals [TSXV:WML] [GR:EJZN] (OTCQB:WMLLF)

Wealth Minerals has a portfolio of lithium assets in Chile, such as 46,200 Has at Atacama, 8,700 Has at Laguna Verde, 6,000 Has at Trinity, 10,500 Has at Five Salars.

No news for the month.

Nevada Lithium Corp. [TSXV:NVLH] [FSE:87K](OTCQB:NVLHF)

Nevada Lithium has an arrangement to own 100% of the Bonnie Claire Project in Nevada, USA.

On March 25 Nevada Lithium announced:

Additional critical mineral potential confirmed with 95.9% cesium and 86.9% rubidium extraction at Bonnie Claire Lithium Project.

Avalon Advanced Materials [TSX:AVL] [GR:OU5] (OTCQX:AVLNF)

Avalon has three projects in Ontario, Canada, and five in total throughout Canada. Avalon’s most advanced project is the Separation Rapids Lithium Project in Ontario with a M& I Petalite Zone Resource of 6.28mt grading 1.37% Li2O, plus an Inferred Resource of 0.94mt at 1.3%. Avalon has a JV with SCR-Sibelco NV ("Sibelco") (60% Sibelco: 40% Avalon) to develop their lithium assets.

No significant news for the month.

Green Technology Metals [ASX:GT1]

On March 16 Green Technology Metals announced:

Half Yearly Report and Accounts...Throughout the half year, we have maintained strict cost discipline...During the period, we achieved several important milestones that strengthen the project’s development pathway. These included successful pilot testwork at EcoPro’s lithium hydroxide facility in South Korea, which validated GT1’s spodumene...

Wildcat Resources [ASX:WC8]

On March 10 Wildcat Resources announced: "Half Yearly Report and Accounts."

Argentina Lithium & Energy Corp. [TSXV: LIT] (OTCQB:PNXLF) (LILIF)

On March 4 Argentina Lithium & Energy announced: "Closing of C$4.4 Million Brokered LIFE Private Placement."

Battery recycling, lithium processing and new cathode technologies

Rock Tech Lithium [CVE:RCK](OTCQX:RCKTF)

On March 3 Rock Tech Lithium announced:

From Guben to Red Rock: Rock Tech and Siemens form Strategic Partnership to transfer proven German lithium conversion expertise to Canada’s next strategic lithium conversion project supporting G7 critical minerals cooperation.

Neometals [ASX:NMT] (NMTAY) (Nasdaq:RDRUY)

On March 20 Neometals announced:

Neometals enters US Lithium‑Potash Brine JV. Neometals has entered into an agreement to acquire a 51% interest in Utah Brine Corporation (“UBC”), the holding company for a lithium and potash (potassium) brine project in Utah’s Paradox Basin, in return for funding up to US$2 million (A$2.82 million) in project costs over three years.

Nano One Materials (TSX: NANO) (OTCPK:NNOMF)

On March 25 Nano One announced:

Nano One highlights recent objectives & reports Fourth Quarter 2025 results.

data.title: Delek Logistics Partners: Distribution Coverage And Leverage Are Red Flags
data.description:
Background

I recently completed an overview of the energy midstream sector based on forward yield, yield coverage, value, profitability, growth, and leverage. Despite a mouth-watering forward yield of 8.53%, strong growth, and favorable valuation, Delek Logistics Partners (DKL) appeared less favorable than most of its peers with the single weakest yield coverage and very high leverage.

My analysis included both corporations and limited partnerships that often define the financial strength behind their yield with their own uniquely defined non-GAAP payout ratio or distribution coverage. In order to make a meaningful comparison, yield coverage was defined in cash flow terms over the last twelve months as:

cash from operations/common and preferred dividends or distributions = yield coverage

As defined, yield coverage neither begins with net income like payout ratio nor accounts for maintenance CapEx like distributable cash flow. It does allow a direct comparison between midstreams regardless of their business structure or non-GAAP accounting practices.

Delek Versus Peers

It's not all about yield, coverage ratio, or leverage; distribution growth counts too. Delek's metrics are plotted beside its four highest-yield peers.

First off, both CrossAmerica Partners (CAPL) and Western Midstream (WES) offer a slightly higher yield, but clearly Delek is committed to rewarding unit holders with thirteen years of consecutive distribution growth - the best record in the group. Delek's 4% 3-year growth rate is somewhat anemic, especially compared to that of WES and Plains All American Pipeline (PAA) at 22% and 19%, respectively. At 1.0X, Delek's coverage ratio is the lowest of the group and substantially weaker than that of both Hess Midstream (HESM) and PAA at 2.8X and 2.3X, respectively. Lastly, Delek's leverage with net debt at 8.0 times EBITDA is the highest in the group.

Overall, Delek's distribution yield and distribution growth history compare favorably with other high-yield midstreams. The company's weak yield coverage and high leverage are clear red flags, but so far we have only looked at a single twelve-month period.

A Wider View of Yield Coverage and Leverage

As previously discussed, midstream operators often employ non-GAAP metrics to quantify and describe their operations. I'm generally suspicious of non-GAAP metrics but have found they can be instructive if they are both appropriate and clearly defined over time. Delek regularly discusses its business in terms of adjusted EBITDA and adjusted distributable cash flow; these two metrics are defined in the company's 4Q25 press release as follows:

  • Adjusted EBITDA - EBITDA adjusted for (i) significant, infrequently occurring transaction costs and (ii) throughput and storage fees associated with the lease component of commercial agreements subject to sales-type lease accounting.
  • Distributable cash flow - calculated as net cash flow from operating activities adjusted for changes in assets and liabilities, maintenance capital expenditures net of reimbursements, sales-type lease receipts, net of income recognized and other adjustments not expected to settle in cash.
  • Distributable cash flow, as adjusted - calculated as distributable cash flow adjusted to exclude significant, infrequently occurring transaction costs.

I would encourage readers to review the non-GAAP reconciliations of adjusted EBITDA and adjusted distributable cash flow presented in Delek's fourth-quarter press releases. These reconciliations are generally consistent over time with the exception of some revenue shifting to adjusted EBITDA as certain lease agreements were reconsidered in late 2025.

Adjusted EBITDA and adjusted distributable cash flow, along with their more universal antecedents, were tabulated from Delek's fourth quarter press releases and are plotted below for YE23 through YE26.

Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted Distributable Cash flow

YE26 revenue is based on Seeking Alpha's consensus estimate, while YE26 adjusted EBITDA of $540M is the midpoint of company guidance. Projected YE26 revenue and adjusted EBITDA are essentially equal to those of YE25; therefore, YE26 adjusted distributable cash flow is assumed to be equal to that of YE25. Delek's revenue has been essentially flat over several years with minimal growth if adjusted EBITDA is considered. Based on consensus estimates and Delek's guidance, both revenue and adjusted EBITDA are expected to be flat in 2026.

Adjusted Coverage Ratio

Considering adjusted distributable cash flow rather than cash from operations, Delek's distribution remains thinly covered. The adjusted YE25 coverage ratio was just 1.24 times distributions. Based on essentially flat revenue and adjusted EBITDA, I expect distributable cash flow to be flat in 2026. The company has increased its quarterly distribution by half a penny over the last several quarters. Assuming this trend continues, the adjusted YE26 coverage ratio is expected to contract further to only 1.21.

Generally, a coverage ratio approaching 2.0 is considered sufficiently robust to not only support distributions but also to finance growth while managing debt and leverage.

Debt and Leverage

Over the last two years, net debt has increased by about $650B to about $2.37B, while adjusted EBITDA has also increased such that debt has remained around 4.4 times adjusted EBITDA. Delek has financed about $300M in acquisitions and growth CapEx in each of the last two years. The company has not provided any guidance on forward growth CapEx, but its Libby gas plant expansion is ongoing in the Delaware Basin; I would not be surprised to see net debt and leverage expand incrementally in 2026.

Delek Versus Peers Revisited

Delek's coverage ratio and leverage are slightly improved based on the company's non-GAAP metrics - adjusted distributable cash flow and adjusted EBITDA.

Note that peer metrics are unchanged, but generally we could expect those metrics to improve as well if their adjusted metrics were considered. Nonetheless, Delek's coverage ratio is still substantially less than that of HESM, PAA, and WES. Delek's unadjusted leverage was by far the worst in the group at 8.0 times, but based on adjusted EBITDA, it falls in the middle of the group with net debt at 4.4 times adjusted EBITDA.

Investor Takeaways

I'm certain that many investors are weak in the knees over Delek's yield and its thirteen years of distribution growth, but sparse distribution coverage and moderately high leverage are red flags demanding a moment of caution. Future distribution growth will be limited unless distribution coverage expands and leverage is reduced. I'm at a wishy-washy hold on Delek; I advise Delek unit holders to carefully consider their positions and watch earnings in early May with particular attention to distribution coverage and leverage.

data.title: TSPY: Collect A Double-Digit Yield From The S&P 500 With Tradeoffs
data.description:
Overview

There is a clear demand in the market for high-yield funds that don't eliminate equity exposure. For instance, there was a record $97B of inflows into equity income ETFs, and I believe that the TappAlpha SPY Growth & Daily Income ETF (TSPY) will see higher inflows if the fund can prove the efficiency of its strategy over time. The fund is still relatively new and unproven, with an inception dating back to August of 2024. Additionally, TSPY has a management fee of 0.68% due to the active nature of the fund.

Looking at the performance over the last twelve months, we can see that TSPY's price has declined by about 1.6%. However, the fund also demonstrated its ability to recover from the initial market decline from about a year ago, since the share price partially participated in some upward momentum of the market. When including all distributions that were paid out to shareholders, the total return jumps up to 13.5% over the same time frame. The main appeal of TSPY is the higher starting dividend yield of about 14.6%, while distributions are paid out on a monthly basis.

Although the fund has demonstrated some positive traits so far, I believe there are some tradeoffs that must be considered. For instance, TSPY underperforms a notable option ETF peer while also capping the upside growth through market rallies. Similarly, the fund hasn't really provided a convincing buffer against the current market decline over the last few quarters. So let's start by taking a look at the underlying strategy that TSPY implements to generate the amplified income levels.

Fund Strategy

According to the latest fund overview, TSPY now has total assets of $245.5M. The fund's primary objective is to provide a high current income while still participating in the capital appreciation of the S&P 500. What I like most about TSPY is that the fund provides direct exposure to the S&P 500 Index by holding the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY). Therefore, the fund is directly exposed to the movement of the index instead of trying to replicate the movements of the index with complicated synthetic option strategies. When looking at the holdings within TSPY, we can see that there is one active options position:

  • CBOE S&P CLL Opt - 03/26 $6,690

As indicated by the holdings, TSPY will write options every single day. By utilizing 0DTE (zero-days-to-expiration) options, this allows managers to adjust its positioning based on the market environment instead of getting locked into a position during an unfavorable market move. The goal is for these options to expire worthless so that the fund can keep the option premiums received, which are used to support the higher distributions.

Furthermore, TSPY implements an OTM (out-of-the-money) option writing strategy to allow some upside movement to be captured. For instance, the current option has a strike price of $6,690, which is above the S&P 500's price of ~$6,541 at the time of this writing. An important distinction to make is that TSPY is writing options on the actual index rather than SPY. This means that the fund is able to capture 100% of the growth of the index up to the strike price.

So far it looks like the strike price sits roughly 2.2% higher than the price of the index. So if the S&P 500 were to rise within that 2.2% window, TSPY will be able to capture all of that upside growth. However, TSPY will experience a capped growth if the index rises above the strike price in a single day.

Performance Expectations

As always, it's important to maintain realistic expectations when holding an option ETF over a longer time frame. For instance, the higher dividend yield doesn't automatically equate to higher total returns over time. Although the operating window is limited, we can see that TSPY's total return of 13.5% is slightly lower than SPY's total return of 15.4% over the same time frame. Additionally, we can see that TSPY's share price hasn't been able to keep up with the strong market rally over the last few quarters. Therefore, investors should anticipate that there's a possibility that most of the total returns will come from the fund's ability to issue large distributions over time.

While the option strategy caps the upside growth at whatever the selected strike price is, there is no layer of protection against downside movements. If the S&P 500 falls by 2% in a single day, TSPY will also decline by the same amount. The only caveat is that any option premiums received can help offset these declines, but it isn't guaranteed that management will always be able to successfully execute its option strategy.

This dynamic means that TSPY isn't structurally capable of rapidly recovering from sharp declines in the market. Therefore, I believe that timing is very important here. For instance, investors that initiated a position on day 1 of the fund's release are still down from a price perspective.

It appears that the most ideal scenario for TSPY would be a sideways market where the S&P 500 is trading choppy over time. The last six months would be a great example of this. Sentiment in the market has shifted, and investors have begun to sell off technology companies due to the threat of AI. Advances in AI have become so rapid that investors are now concerned about the moat around software companies, which caused a selloff among the S&P 500. Since the index is most heavily comprised of technology companies, SPY has traded sideways over the last six months.

When comparing TSPY's total return against SPY over the last six months, we can see that TSPY slightly outperformed and still maintains a small positive total return. The included option strategy helps generate option premiums that can slightly offset sideways markets, while traditional ETF holders are stuck waiting for an eventual recovery. I think that under normal circumstances TSPY may be able to provide a wider outperformance gap during sideways markets, but the volatility over the last few weeks has been extreme. We have rising tensions with Iran, uncertainty around interest rates, and increasing unemployment rates.

Risks

However, there are some consequences that must be considered. Besides the capped upside growth, the fund's structure also underperforms a peer like the NEOS S&P 500 High Income ETF (SPYI). SPYI offers a higher dividend yield of about 12.4%, but this is because of the structural differences. While SPYI also writes OTM options against the S&P 500 index, the fund chooses to hold the equities individually that make up the index, rather than choosing to hold an ETF like SPY. The choice to hold individual holdings means that SPYI has a greater potential to participate in the growth of its top holdings.

When comparing the performance between TSPY and SPYI, we can see that SPYI outperforms on a YTD basis. Although SPYI also saw its share price decline, the fund held up slightly better than TSPY. Additionally, SPYI's higher distribution yield means that the total return is a bit better than TSPY as well. Unfortunately, both of the funds have a limited operating history, so performance comparisons are limited. However, I do wish to complete a more in-depth comparison in the future.

Although an ETF like SPY is thought to be an extremely efficient way to get diversity across the market, we must not forget that the index is heavily skewed towards technology companies. For instance, ~32.56% of SPY's total assets are allocated towards technology holdings, and its top holdings make up more than a third of its assets. If the selloff of the index continues, TSPY's performance may continue to be disappointing. Furthermore, NAV erosion remains a threat if the fund fails to generate an efficient level of option premiums that can support payouts. When you combine these systems, NAV erosion is an inevitability over time:

  • Capped upside but full downside dynamic.
  • Mixed volatility levels that may not lead to high option premiums.
  • Using return of capital.

In order to keep an emphasis on tax efficiency, TSPY may use return of capital distributions to support payouts. Return of capital distributions aren't directly taxed as income, but they reduce an investor's cost basis, which allows taxes to be deferred until the time of sale. The caveat is that a return of capital distribution directly impacts the underlying NAV over time, so overreliance on ROC during a period of declines will exacerbate the issue of a declining share price. For reference, the most updated Section 19(a) notice indicates that 100% of the distributions paid YTD were classified as return of capital.

Dividend Utility

However, I would also argue that the use of return of capital is great for investors that want to limit their overall tax liability. Since this form of distribution doesn't have any immediate tax impacts, it's great for long-term investors that want to use TSPY as an accompanying fund to an already diverse portfolio of assets. Since return of capital distributions reduce an investor's cost basis, it is technically possible to maintain a position long enough for your cost basis to reach $0 per share. At this point, all distributions received would be classified as long-term capital gains, which are still qualified dividends and have favorable tax treatment.

Although the fund's history is short, TSPY has done a great job at keeping payouts within a consistent range since its inception. However, I would caution that there's always a risk for payouts to trend lower depending on market conditions. If the S&P 500 experiences a prolonged downturn, the underlying NAV can erode over time. If the NAV erodes, the fund has fewer assets working on its behalf to produce option premiums, which means that payouts will need to get adjusted downward.

Despite the potential risk, the fund's ability to maintain a consistent range of distributions should still be commended. While I wouldn't anticipate any sort of traditional dividend growth to take place, I believe that the consistent payouts enable investors to create their own dividend growth by reinvesting all payouts back into accumulating more shares or investing new capital on a consistent basis. Furthermore, the monthly frequency may be great for investors that want to use dividends as a way to support lifestyle expenses.

Takeaway

In conclusion, I believe that TSPY is only a hold right now. The fund's operating strategy can efficiently generate a consistent stream of distributions every month, but the fund still underperforms SPYI so far. I believe it has to do with the decision to hold SPY instead of the actual holdings that make up the S&P 500 Index. The fund caps upside growth, but there are no systems in place to protect against downside risks. If the market continues to trend downward through 2026, TSPY hasn't done a great job at providing a buffer against these unfavorable market conditions.

data.title: Coveo Solutions May Be Cheap If AI Growth Can Finally Improve Margins
data.description:
Coveo Solutions Inc. (CVO:CA) is a software company that offers a Software-as-a-Service platform, which they provide through the cloud with integrated AI capabilities. These solutions are designed to support clients and employees in e-commerce, customer service, websites, and workplace applications. Also, CVO's main product is called the AI-Relevance Platform, which is a system that processes and connects enterprise data with LLMs from OpenAI, Amazon Bedrock, and others. They offer this AI service through secure and personalized channels. And, for the most part, I believe this approach is well-positioned to thrive with the ongoing disruptive AI wave in SaaS. That's why I ultimately lean bullish on the stock after its recent pullback.

Enterprise SaaS AI

Coveo Solutions Inc. is a software company that develops AI-powered solutions through a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) cloud-based platform. CVO was founded back in 2004, and it is currently headquartered in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. CVO recently had a strong quarterly report as well, showing more customer deployments, potential new government contracts, and future product launches. So, I thought it was worthwhile to write an analysis of their future prospects at this point.

As a quick overview, CVO's portfolio includes the AI-Relevance Platform, which has several capabilities. Concretely, it offers AI agents for data retrieval, generative answering, AI recommendations, personalization, analytics, and security. These features are delivered in four business solutions for commerce, customer service, websites, and workplace applications with AI support to employees. CVO's platform is suited to sectors where service workflows are complex. In these environments, the company solutions can unify service knowledge, diagnostic content, and other data into a single AI layer to improve uptime and support service teams, which in turn reduces servicing costs.

CVO's Growth Strategy and Prospects

With that in mind, I believe CVO's main differentiator lies in their platform's several value layers. You see, they have different kinds of data sources, including cloud, structured, and unstructured data. The processed data connects securely with client systems. This layer is followed by a knowledge layer that contextualizes and personalizes enterprise content, which is then indexed while preserving enterprise permissions. Later, that content is transformed into semantic representations that are fed into several AI models and a closed-loop learning layer that tracks user behavior to improve results over time.

In that sense, I believe CVO's value proposition is less about using underlying large language models accessed through providers like OpenAI and Amazon Bedrock, among others. The actual value lies in CVO, which processes company data with high security standards in a way that offers useful information for customers. Companies don't want to send sensitive internal information into a general-purpose AI workflow, as this could obviously compromise their operations or expose them to hacker attack vectors.

That's why I think CVO's business should remain viable despite the ongoing AI SaaS-pocalypse. After all, CVO offers business customers the ability to control their data with a refined security layer that also produces valuable data insights. Similarly, I think CVO's services allow for highly personalized AI capabilities that deliver context-aware retrieval and ranking across their customers' workflows. This flexible approach means they can improve the quality of support for customers and employees across several industries.

However, note that in the long run, CVO's overarching strategy is to become a complementary service to Salesforce's (CRM) Agentforce and other AI agent systems. CVO's management believes this will position them as a vital retrieval layer for customer management and support service workflows. Plus, CVO's recent strong Q3 2026 showed over 80 subscriptions and deployments with current customers. And they also signed a non-binding MOU to explore how CVO's AI could modernize the Canadian government services, with potential large-scale opportunities. So, business-wise, I feel CVO has ample room for future growth across different niches.

Valuation and Risk Analysis

Now, from a valuation perspective, CVO trades at a $411.7 million market cap. Its December 2025 financials show its balance sheet holds $100.8 million in cash, against no financial debt aside from regular operating liabilities. Note that its book value was only $96.6 million, but that's mostly due to its substantial accumulated losses of $704.5 million. Still, that would imply a manageable P/B of 4.3. So despite being heavily punished by its accumulated deficit, CVO's P/B isn't that expensive.

Moreover, CVO is projected to generate $229.6 million by 2027, which prices the stock at a reasonable forward P/S of 1.8. And, if we account for its cash balances, I estimate CVO's EV at $310.9 million. This would imply a much more compelling forward EV/S of 1.4. Thus, I would argue the stock's fundamentals offer enough value at these levels.

Naturally, a key caveat to these optimistic valuation metrics is that CVO remains structurally unprofitable. As of Q4 2025, they reported an EBIT loss of -$6.3 million, which I calculate implies a Q4 2025 EBIT margin of -16.5%. And this was worse than Q4 2024's EBIT loss of -$5.4 million, which at the time represented a Q4 2024 EBIT margin of -15.9%. Essentially, we continue seeing a deteriorating EBIT margin YoY, despite the fact that YoY Q4 2025 added approximately $4.1 million in quarterly revenues.

Fortunately, CVO seems to be self-sustainable despite these concerning operating margin trends. I calculate that their nine-month cash burn was approximately $4.0 million (as of December 2025). Note that I got that figure by simply adding CVO's nine-month cash flows from operations and CAPEX. But that number suggests a yearly burn rate of roughly $5.3 million, which looks quite manageable relative to their $100.8 million in cash reserves. So, I think dilution/financing risks should be minimal for now.

Therefore, at this point, the main question is whether or not they'll be able to improve their margins rapidly enough to justify a higher valuation. Here, two factors help me lean bullish on the stock. First, their core SaaS platform is actually growing at 15% YoY (excluding Qubit, which is now a negligible drag going forward). And secondly, CVO's gross margins are incredibly high at 78%, which leads me to believe that as their topline grows, operating and net margins will probably expand.

Of course, that's an inherently speculative assumption, so it will be key to watch if the following quarters show improving EBIT margins. And if that doesn't materialize, then I think the stock's upside potential may be capped in the near term. But on the flip side, if we see margins grow alongside revenues, then I suspect the market might reprice the stock higher this year. Aside from this, it's also worth mentioning that CVO is a somewhat thinly traded stock across different exchanges, with roughly 162.5 thousand shares in consolidated volume. So, the current $4.4 PPS implies a shallow trading liquidity of $715.0 thousand, which means it has shallow trading liquidity and suggests even small buy/sell orders could keep CVO volatile for the foreseeable future.

Conclusion: Worth Speculating

Overall, I think CVO's approach to AI and SaaS is promising and has solid long-term growth prospects. The main concern is whether or not they'll be able to actually offer their services at profitable operating and net margins. I believe that as their topline scales up, margins will improve accordingly. But until their future financial reports corroborate this bull case, I can only rate CVO as a speculative "Buy." Still, after the stock's recent 53% pullback from its 2025 highs, I feel the downside risk is much more muted compared to CVO's potential upside if they succeed. Just make sure you keep your position sizing manageable.

  • Alphabet (GOOGL), Cooper, and Salesforce (CRM) were the top contributors in the quarter while Arm Holdings (ARM), Microsoft (MSFT), and Meta (META) Platforms were the largest detractors.
  • We initiated a new position in Broadcom (AVGO).
  • The portfolio's relative valuation is at its most attractive level since the inception of the firm. Meanwhile, cyclicals trade at peak multiples just as CapEx growth is poised to slow. As such, we believe there is an asymmetrically favorable risk-reward opportunity in the portfolio presently.

Performance

The SGA U.S. Large Cap Growth portfolio returned 0.3% (gross) and -0.4% (net) in the 4 th quarter compared to 1.1% for the Russell 1000 Growth Index and 2.7% for the S&P 500 portfolio. Despite trailing for the quarter, we were pleased to see the portfolio's relative performance improve as market volatility picked up in November and December, outpacing the Russell 1000 Growth Index by more than 3% during the second half of the quarter. Further broadening of market leadership moving forward should be positive for relative performance.

2025 was one of the most challenging years for the portfolio since the firm was founded in 2003. While the portfolio delivered strong relative performance during the market's tariff-induced correction in March-April, the historically strong rebound from the market lows on April 8 th proved much more difficult for our style as lower-quality, speculative, and cyclically sensitive stocks outperformed massively while companies with the quality characteristics we seek lagged. For the year the portfolio returned 3.7% (gross) and 0.7% (net), lagging the 18.6% and 17.9% returns delivered by the Russell 1000 Growth and S&P 500 Indices. Despite the relatively modest and disappointing return for the year, underlying fundamentals remained solid with earnings for the portfolio compounding 12% year-over-year. The Russell 1000 Growth and S&P 500 Indices grew earnings by 16% and 13% for the year, well ahead of long-term averages. In addition to the strong growth in earnings Index returns were supported by another year of valuation expansion, which by our calculations accounted for nearly half of broad market performance over the past three years. In contrast, the SGA portfolio suffered from further multiple contraction during the year as capital continued to flee stable growth companies in search of more exciting growth stories. These dynamics have resulted in the portfolio's relative valuation compared to the Russell 1000 Growth Index now at the lowest (and therefore most attractive) level since the inception of the firm. This makes us very excited about the opportunity over the coming years, especially since cyclicals are trading at peak multiples just as AI capital expenditures are poised to slow.

One of our most deeply held investment beliefs is perhaps best articulated by Benjamin Graham: "In the short run the market is a voting machine, but in the long run, it is a weighing machine." This year, the market clearly voted in favor of companies most sensitive to the AI capital expenditure boom, rate cuts, and an improving macro-economic backdrop. In the long run, however, we have confidence in the weighing machine rewarding the steady and reliable high-quality compounders found in the SGA portfolio, which despite not being rewarded today continue to compound earnings and cash flows attractively.

A Narrow and Cyclical Momentum Market

2025 was one of the most extreme momentum markets of the past seven decades with a historic rally following “Liberation Day” and the subsequent market bottom on April 8 th . The last nine months of the year were characterized by market leadership concentrated in lower-quality, more speculative, and cyclically sensitive stocks. Non-profitable tech stocks were among the market darlings while companies with defensive characteristics, stable margins, and reliable growth prospects underperformed by a wide margin. The cyclical trade was buoyed by the massive spending going into AI infrastructure, which accounted for roughly a quarter of U.S. GDP growth through the third quarter, leading investors to chase immediate beneficiaries such as semiconductor and AI capital equipment stocks. Cyclical industries performed best during the year and had the highest proportion of outperformers given excitement around the AI infrastructure boom, interest rate cuts, upcoming tax refunds, and incentives to bring back domestic production and manufacturing for corporates in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA). In contrast, companies and industries with less cyclicality and those perceived to be in the crosshairs of AI, lagged by a wide margin and had the highest proportion of underperformers. These dynamics weighed heavily on several holdings within the portfolio, including ServiceNow (NOW) and Salesforce, which saw their stocks decline by 20%+ for the year despite delivering solid financial results while having attractive future growth prospects.

Fueling the momentum rally are expectations for strong and accelerating growth in 2026-27. While we acknowledge that growth rates are likely to remain strong in 2026, we are more skeptical about consensus expectations for 2027. As it stands, the implied 4-year growth rate from 2023-2027 of ~18% for the Russell 1000 Growth Index is roughly 2 standard deviations above the 25-year average of 9% average annual growth.

The strong growth backdrop and high optimism around future growth has supported significant valuation expansion in recent years, which has been a big tailwind for broad-market performance. However, if we dissect the numbers, we find important distinctions between which types of companies have benefited from these trends, and which have not. The charts below from Empirical Research Partners illustrate the divergence between companies with stable fundamentals, which have seen relative valuations plummet to the lowest levels in decades, and cyclical stocks like capital equipment which are valued at historically high levels. We suspect many investors are currently valuing cyclical stocks as linear compounders, neglecting their inherent unpredictability and sensitivity to the business and economic cycle.

AI Capital Expenditures Update

Our view is that the current trend of AI-related CapEx growth is unsustainable and will moderate moving forward. There are three structural constraints we expect will limit growth in spending moving forward: power availability, skilled labor shortages, and capital availability. These constraints are central to SGA's view that AI CapEx growth rates will decelerate and lead to slowing of cyclical momentum rather than collapsing demand outright.

A good portion of the current CapEx spending by the hyperscalers ( Microsoft, Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet, Meta ) is directed toward securing power generation, grid access, and data center real estate – assets with multi-decade useful lives. These are long-term investments meant to ensure scarce and strategically important resources that are deemed necessary for future competition. Recent transactions, such as Alphabet's acquisition of Intersect, underscore the growing importance of power access. While such deals help at the margin, they are unlikely to fully resolve power bottlenecks, which we expect to persist for years. The shortages of skilled tradesmen such as electricians, welders, and plumbers in the construction industry, estimated to be around 400,000+ by The Associated Builders and Contractors trade group, is also pushing out project timelines and will make it harder to keep up with current demand for power generation and data center construction. According to insights gleaned from a recent research trip to the country's preeminent utility conference, lead times for new power installation are being extended from 2-3 years to 4-6 years due to labor shortages.

Lastly, with hyperscaler CapEx spending having reached historically high proportions of revenues and operating cash flow, the lack of incremental capital availability to be deployed towards CapEx is an important factor which we expect will constrain the rate of growth on future spending from these companies.

Capital Expended: U.S. Hyperscalers Forecast to Utilize 90% of Operating Cash Flows

While the CapEx is still filtering down various cyclical industries, further upward growth revisions are challenging. And, therefore, purely cyclical sectors whose growth rate is exposed to the growth rate of hyperscaler CapEx will have a shorter runway of growth left.

We acknowledge that higher levels of CapEx expenditures have shifted the hyperscalers into a more capital-intensive business model. However, as return on invested capital remains high along with 30-40% operating margins and strong cash flow generation across their businesses, we still view their future return potential as attractive. That said, we are actively monitoring key risks such as the select use of off-balance sheet financing, customer concentration and revenue quality, technological obsolescence given rapid advancements in chip technology, utilization rates, and depreciation schedules and their impact on earnings. We view risks across these dimensions and their possible impact to future growth expectations to be low currently.

Finally, our view remains that the most attractive long-term AI opportunities reside not with companies exposed to cyclical swings, but with businesses that are building long-term value. In 2025, portfolio holdings Nvidia (NVDA) and Alphabet benefited significantly from their positions in the AI ecosystem. However, many AI beneficiaries have yet to be recognized by the market, providing significant latent potential in the portfolio. Examples of this latent potential include software companies with proprietary data, embedded customer relationships, and integrated workflows that can monetize AI through high-margin applications without bearing the full burden of infrastructure investment, positioning these businesses to benefit from the capex boom while maintaining strong returns on capital.

Largest Contributors

Alphabet

Alphabet was a top contributor during the quarter, driven by accelerating growth across its core businesses and strong execution in AI-enabled products. Search queries and revenues both accelerated, challenging concerns about competitive threats from AI chat tools and supporting the case for continued innovation-driven market expansion. YouTube and Cloud also posted robust growth, with Cloud backlog and revenues benefiting from strong enterprise demand for AI solutions. Alphabet's disciplined margin management and diversified revenue streams, alongside strategic investments in AI and cloud, position it well for continued growth. Despite ongoing regulatory and competitive pressures, Alphabet's leadership in search, video, and cloud, combined with its ability to monetize a loyal global user base, should support double-digit earnings and revenue growth in the years ahead. We raised the target to an above-average weight position.

Cooper

Cooper was a top contributor during the quarter after reporting good operating results and announcing several shareholder-friendly developments. In terms of operating results, the company demonstrated progress in alleviating supply constraints for its premium daily silicone hydrogel contact lenses, announced new private label contract wins in the U.S. and Europe, and delivered innovation in myopia control and multifocal lens platforms. In addition, CooperSurgical showed sequential improvement, despite some macro headwinds in Asia Pacific.

In terms of shareholder-friendly developments, we were pleased to see progress made by the company to better align with long-term value creation, such as incorporating total shareholder return and free cash flow metrics in management compensation incentives after receiving feedback from shareholders such as ourselves. In addition, long-term free cash flow guidance was raised. Lastly, the company announced a review of strategic options for its women's health business and the addition of Steris CEO Emeritus Walter Rosebrough, a business leader we know well and hold in high regard, to its board of directors. These positive developments are consistent with feedback we have provided management as part of our ongoing engagement with the company, including a recent meeting with newly promoted board Chairwoman Colleen Jay.

While the company faced some near-term challenges, Cooper's broad product portfolio, strong market position, and ongoing innovation provide attractive long-term growth opportunities and we believe the company is taking active steps in the present to realize those opportunities. We trimmed the position on strength following the stock's recovery during the quarter and maintained an average weight.

Salesforce

Salesforce was a top contributor during the quarter, as the company delivered solid quarterly results and demonstrated clear signs of growth stabilization and renewed momentum. Subscription revenue growth and current remaining performance obligation (cRPO) both exceeded guidance, while management is targeting revenue acceleration over the next 12-18 months, supported by increased competitiveness in the SMB segment and the expiration of pandemic-era shelfware deals. The company's AI and Data Cloud offerings are gaining traction, with Agentforce annual recurring revenue growing by over 300% and Data Cloud annual recurring revenue more than doubling, demonstrating a measurable growth contribution from AI streams and deepening customer engagement. Salesforce's broad suite of integrated front-office applications, trusted brand, and strong renewal rates continue to drive revenue growth, while disciplined capital allocation and aggressive share repurchases enhance shareholder returns. We believe Salesforce's integration into customer business processes, large installed base, wide product set, and Slack engagement position it well to compete effectively in the Agentic AI era, supporting low-teens earnings growth over the next three years. During the quarter, we maintained an average weight and added to the position through the sale of other higher multiple software holdings.

Largest Detractors

Arm Holdings

Arm Holdings was a detractor during the quarter, despite a strong fiscal Q2 earnings report with revenue up 34% and profit 43%. Royalty revenue grew 21%, driven by triple digits data center growth and higher smartphone royalty from compute subsystem (CSS) customers. License revenue increased 56%, or 17% excluding related-party design service fees from SoftBank. The next quarter guidance for sales growth of 25% and profit up 20% was also ahead of expectations. However, the company faced several headwinds that weighed on investor sentiment. Elevated R&D spending, mostly related to Arm's design service for SoftBank, will impact Arm's margin outlook for next year. In addition, SoftBank is borrowing against its Arm investment to finance its investment into OpenAI, which raised concerns over forced selling of Arm shares if SoftBank does not secure sufficient funding for its investment commitments. Despite these near-term challenges, we remain confident in Arm's growth outlook, and we expect Arm to provide greater clarity over its chip project with SoftBank in 2026. We maintained a below-average weight position during the quarter.

Microsoft

Microsoft, a global leader in enterprise software and cloud services, was a detractor during the quarter, despite an overall solid quarterly report. Azure growth of 39% came in slightly below expectations, and guidance for the next quarter was weaker than expected, which management asserted was due to supply constraints.

The company's partnership with OpenAI has accelerated investments in infrastructure and the ultimate return on these investments remains difficult to quantify. At the same time, the adoption of Copilot has been lackluster, and Microsoft remains dependent on OpenAI for the core AI features. In our view, the returns on the infrastructure investments should be exciting over time. We also remain confident that Microsoft can develop the LLM expertise in-house or partner with other companies as OpenAI is unlikely to maintain the level of dominance it has had thus far in the AI journey.

Microsoft's business model is anchored by substantial pricing power, high switching costs, and a growing base of recurring revenues as more products transition to cloud and Software-as-a-Service models. While elevated capital expenditures and evolving market dynamics present challenges, Microsoft's strong customer base, leadership in cloud and AI, and disciplined execution position it well for double-digit earnings and revenue growth as the benefits of these investments materialize. We maintained an above-average weight position.

Meta

Meta was a detractor during the quarter, despite delivering strong top-line results, as the company's robust revenue growth of 26% and accelerating user engagement were overshadowed by a significant increase in capital expenditure and operating expense guidance. While engagement and ad performance continued to improve, the company raised its capex outlook again, with expenses expected to grow over 30%, outpacing revenue revisions and pressuring near-term profitability. Despite these headwinds, Meta's leadership in social discovery, performance advertising, and its ability to productize AI for consumers position the company well for strong double-digit revenue growth over the next three years. While the company's long-term positioning in AI remains compelling, we lowered the target weight to an average weight on concerns about heavy increases in AI capital expenditures, later trimming the position back to target.

Portfolio Activity

We initiated a new position in Broadcom. Positions in Netflix (NFLX), Nike (NKE), and Salesforce were added to on weakness while positions in American Express (AXP), Cooper, Grainger (GWW), Intuit (INTU), Meta, and ServiceNow were trimmed.

Purchases

Broadcom

During the quarter, we initiated a below-average weight position in leading semiconductor and infrastructure software company Broadcom. Broadcom's ongoing transformation from a provider of custom ASICs to a full rack-scale solutions vendor, including scale-up networking, marks a significant improvement in its strategic positioning compared to earlier in the year. This strategic evolution has been validated by recent product launches, such as the Scale-up Ethernet solution, and committed orders from major customers like Anthropic. Broadcom is also diversifying its customer base, expanding from three to five qualified customers with purchase commitments, and accelerating production volume timelines for future prospective clients. The shift in AI demand from training to inferencing is creating more visible and stable revenue streams, as inferencing workloads have grown rapidly, driven by innovations in long context windows and agentic workloads.

Broadcom's business model is supported by strong pricing power, recurring revenues, and robust growth opportunities. The company maintains industry-leading margins, with semiconductor gross margin around 70% and software gross margin near 90%, leveraging high technology barriers and strong customer relationships. Recurring revenue is underpinned by long-term AI semiconductor engagements and high-retention infrastructure software subscriptions. As AI spending shifts toward inference, revenue visibility and stability improve, backed by real use cases and multi-year customer commitments. Broadcom's growth is driven by rising AI demand, as major labs and hyperscalers adopt custom ASICs for both training and inference. The company's ability to integrate a full systems solution from ASICs to networking provides an advantage over other vendors, and its strategy of building sustainable franchises ensures resilience against market and technology shifts. While competition and customer concentration remain risks, Broadcom is actively expanding its custom ASIC engagements to improve diversification. Broadcom's strategic evolution and focus on organic growth and AI position it well for long-term stability and profitability, supporting over 20% revenue and earnings growth.

Sales

None

Outlook: Conviction Through Extremes

While the past year has been tough for the portfolio, we are comforted by the resilient growth opportunities across our portfolio holdings and the now historically attractive relative valuation of the portfolio. We believe the setup moving forward is very attractive as the portfolio is well-positioned to benefit from a softening of the extreme momentum dynamics that have dominated U.S. markets over the past 2-3 years and a broadening of market leadership. Our philosophy, process, and approach remain steadfast. We will continue to invest in high-quality companies where we see attractively valued long-term durable growth – an approach that has delivered good returns with lower levels of risk over time. As AI capital expenditure growth inevitably slows, we find it unlikely that the market will continue to ignore the wonderful high-quality businesses in our portfolio and are excited about the opportunity ahead for our clients.

Thank you for your patience and partnership – we wish you all the best for 2026.
